qa_pairs.question: What systems were not affected by the electrical maintenance outage that included Béluga, Narval, and Juno (non-HA)?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga cloud instances and Juno cloud instances in the high availability zone were not affected.

qa_pairs.question: Was Béluga and Narval storage accessible during the electrical maintenance outage affecting compute nodes and Juno cloud (non-HA)?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Béluga and Narval storage remained accessible through Globus and the login nodes of each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the Cedar cluster outage from March 31 to April 2, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was required to perform power modifications as part of preparations for bringing new equipment online.

qa_pairs.question: What was the operational status of the Cedar cluster during the March 31 to April 2, 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar cluster was completely unavailable, meaning users could not log in or run jobs.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs running on the Cedar cluster during the March 31 to April 2, 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Any jobs running at the time of the outage were terminated and had to be re-submitted once the cluster came back online.

qa_pairs.question: Did the Cedar Cloud remain operational during the March 31 to April 2, 2025 outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar Cloud remained operational during this period.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham data centre shutdown originally scheduled?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham data centre shutdown was originally scheduled from December 7, 2024, to January 3, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the Graham data centre shut down?
qa_pairs.answer: Ongoing renovations required a complete data centre shutdown.

qa_pairs.question: Were Graham cluster services, storage, and cloud services available during the data centre shutdown starting December 7, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: No, all Graham cluster services, storage, and cloud services were entirely unavailable during this time.

qa_pairs.question: Was the Graham data centre outage extended?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the outage was extended due to some delays.

qa_pairs.question: What was the extended end date for the Graham data centre outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was extended to February 24, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find updated information about the extended Graham data centre outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Updated information about the extended Graham data centre outage is available on https://status.alliancecan.ca as of January 28, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the purpose of the temporary reduction in Béluga and Narval compute nodes from January 13 to February 14, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The reduction was for performance and stability tests on Rorqual.

qa_pairs.question: Which compute nodes were affected by the temporary reduction starting January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: All Béluga compute nodes and about half of Narval compute nodes were shut down.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the initial shutdown for Béluga and Narval compute nodes in January 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The initial shutdown was from 8 a.m. on January 13 until 12 p.m. (noon) on January 31, 2025 (EST).

qa_pairs.question: Were login nodes and data access operational during the temporary reduction on Béluga and Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, login nodes and data access remained operational.

qa_pairs.question: How many Narval nodes were shut down during the temporary reduction starting January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Approximately 50% of nodes from each category (CPU, GPU, and large memory) on Narval were shut down.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to Béluga Storage during the temporary reduction for Rorqual tests?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga Storage (/lustre01, /lustre02, /lustre03, /lustre04 of Beluga) was mounted to Narval during the shutdown time.

qa_pairs.question: Were Béluga and Juno cloud instances affected by the temporary reduction for Rorqual tests?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Béluga and Juno cloud instances were unaffected.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs scheduled on Béluga after 8 a.m. on January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs on Béluga scheduled to complete after 8 a.m. on January 13 remained queued until the cluster resumed.

qa_pairs.question: What was the updated status of Narval's compute capacity as of January 30, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: As of January 30, 2025, Narval's compute capacity was at 100% until February 6, then again at 30% for the last Rorqual tests.

qa_pairs.question: When were Béluga and Narval expected to return to 100% capacity after the temporary reduction?
qa_pairs.answer: Béluga and Narval were expected to be back to 100% capacity on February 14.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find updated information about the Béluga and Narval temporary reduction status?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find updated information on https://status.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: When were Niagara and Mist compute nodes shut down on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Niagara and Mist compute nodes were shut down on January 22, 2025, from 8 AM to 5 PM EST.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the Niagara and Mist compute node shutdown on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The shutdown was to support ongoing system improvements and the integration with the new Trillium system.

qa_pairs.question: Which systems remained available during the January 22, 2025, Niagara and Mist outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The login nodes, file systems, and the HPSS system remained available.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to submitted jobs during the Niagara and Mist outage on January 22, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler held jobs that were submitted until the maintenance had finished.

qa_pairs.question: What was the purpose of the Cedar compute cluster shutdown from January 13 to January 21, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: The shutdown was in preparation for infrastructure renewal.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to jobs submitted to the Cedar cluster during the January 13 to January 21, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs submitted to the cluster queued and may have started running if they could complete before the shutdown; otherwise, they remained in the queue until the cluster was fully operational on January 21.

qa_pairs.question: What was to happen with the Cedar /scratch filesystem during the January 13 to January 21, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar /scratch filesystem was to be migrated to new storage.

qa_pairs.question: What action were users advised to take regarding important data on Cedar before the January 13, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were advised to move any important data immediately to their /project, /nearline, or /home directory.

qa_pairs.question: Did Cedar cloud remain operational during the January 13 to January 21, 2025, outage?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Cedar cloud remained operational during this period.

qa_pairs.question: What was the reason for the Niagara outage from November 25 to November 26, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: A full power shutdown was taking place for main panel upgrades ahead of Trillium cluster setup.

qa_pairs.question: What Niagara services were affected by the November 25-26, 2024, power shutdown?
qa_pairs.answer: All Niagara services, including the cluster and scheduler, paused during this time.

qa_pairs.question: What was the scheduler's behaviour for jobs during the Niagara power shutdown on November 25-26, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler held jobs that could not finish before the start of the shutdown.

qa_pairs.question: What were users encouraged to do with their jobs before the Niagara power shutdown?
qa_pairs.answer: Users were encouraged to submit smaller, short-duration jobs to optimize idle node usage before the maintenance began.

qa_pairs.question: What systems at the SciNet Datacenter were unavailable from November 7 to November 8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: All systems and storage at the SciNet Datacenter, including Niagara, Mist, HPSS, Rouge, Teach, JupyterHub, and Balam, were unavailable.

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration of the SciNet Datacenter outage on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The outage was from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET.

qa_pairs.question: Why was the SciNet Datacenter outage necessary on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: This outage was necessary for installing new electrical equipment (UPS) as part of a systems refresh.

qa_pairs.question: How did the scheduler handle jobs during the SciNet Datacenter outage on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: The scheduler paused jobs unable to finish before the shutdown.

qa_pairs.question: What advice was given to users regarding jobs prior to the SciNet Datacenter outage on November 7-8, 2024?
qa_pairs.answer: Users could prioritize short jobs to utilize otherwise idle nodes prior to maintenance.
